zh.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden)於1374年之前與Baron William de Cawarden在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden)于1374年之前与Baron William de Cawarden在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: (wife
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: either a topographic or occupational name for someone who lived by or who was in charge of some kind of mechanical contrivance (a windlass, perhaps, or an animal snare) or possibly a fish trap, or a habitational name for someone who came from a place called with the same word (Middle English wile), such as Wyld Farm, in Hampstead Norris (Berks), Monkton Wild (formerly in Wootton Fitzpaine, Dorset), or Wyle Cop in Shrewsbury (Shropshire).
English: perhaps from Middle English wild ‘wild’ (see Wild ).
Americanized form of German   Weil  .

variants.name: Wild
meanings: English: from Middle English wilde ‘wild, violent’ (Old English wilde), hence a nickname for a man of violent and undisciplined character, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a patch of overgrown uncultivated land (from Middle English wilde (noun) ‘wild place, wasteland’).
English: variant of Wile , with excrescent -d.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): cognate of 1 above, from Middle High German wilde, wilt, German wild ‘wild’, also used in the sense ‘strange, foreign’, and therefore in some cases a nickname for an incomer.

variants.name: Wileman
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Leicestershire): occupational name perhaps from Middle English wyle ‘fish-trap’ (see Wile ) + man ‘man’, denoting someone who made or used fish traps, or a variant of Willman .

variants.name: Wiles
meanings: English: either a nickname from Middle English wiles (Old English wīlas) ‘tricks, cunning strategies’, or a variant of Wile 1 or 2, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Weil
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Gunther, Hans, Herta, Lothar, Manfred, Arno, Dieter, Elfriede, Fritz.

South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places so named in Baden, Bavaria, and Württemberg, from Latin villa ‘country house, estate’ (later used of a group of houses forming a settlement). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Wile 3.
History: This is one of the old Ashkenazic surnames. In Eastern Europe, it was used by rabbinical families well before the mass adoption of surnames by local Jews at the turn of the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Memoli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ciro, Francesco, Luciano, Natale, Nino.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a pet form of thepersonal name Guglielmo, Italian form of William .

variants.name: McElligott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brigid, Brendan, Seamus.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uileagóid, a patronymic from a pet form of the Gaelic form of the personal name William .

variants.name: McWilliam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain, Morag.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , also Anglicized as Williamson in Scotland. The name is thought to be mainly Scottish in Ulster, though there has been some confusion with McQuillan in County Down. In Connacht, McWilliam was a branch of Burke .

variants.name: Wilcock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: McWilliams
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , with excrescent English -s. In Ireland it sometimes represents a partial Gaelicization of Welsh Williams .

variants.name: Lemmo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Donato, Gino.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Lemmo, a pet form of Guglielmo, an equivalent of William . Compare Lembo .

variants.name: Wilk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wilkin, a pet form of William introduced to England and Scotland by Flemings and Picards after the Norman Conquest.
North German: variant of   Wilken  .

variants.name: Gillam
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .
Welsh: Anglicized form of   Gwilliam  .

variants.name: Lembo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmelo, Domenic, Pietro, Alfio, Amelio, Antonio, Biagio, Dante, Giovani, Giovanni.
Italian (southern): from a hypercorrected form of Lemmo, a pet form of Guglièlmo, an equivalent of William .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

en.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden) married Baron William de Cawarden before 1374, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden) a épousé Baron William de Cawarden avant 1374, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden) hat vor 1374 in England Baron William de Cawarden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden) lei sposato Baron William de Cawarden prima del 1374, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Cawarthyn,,Cheshire,England: イギリスチェシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden)は1374年以前にイギリスでBaron William de Cawardenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden)(은)는 1374년 이전 영국에서 Baron William de Cawarden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden) casou-se com Baron William de Cawarden antes 1374, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden) вышла замуж за человека по имени Baron William de Cawarden перед 1374 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Female Unknown (wife of William de Cawarden) se casó con Baron William de Cawarden antes de 1374, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
